evidence: Overlay state ownership is duplicated across multiple UI components: `CreativePartnerPane`, `InstallInstructionsModal`, `DescriptionModal`, `InfoTooltip`, and `ConfirmDialog` each wrap controlled `open/onOpenChange` props with `useOverlayState`, creating repeated boundary plumbing instead of a single architectural convention for controlled overlays.

Type ownership is split between transport compatibility and domain model surfaces: `src/types/generationRow.ts` re-exports `GenerationRowDto as GenerationRow` as a compatibility facade while domain types (for example `src/domains/generation/types/shot.ts`) reference a different `GenerationRow` source, which makes top-level type authority less predictable.

Two high-import shared entry points (`handleError.ts` and `integrations/supabase/client.ts`) aggregate cross-cutting runtime behavior; while each file is small, they become architectural hubs and increase ripple risk when behavior changes.

No test hits were found for `buildOrchestrationContract` and `runBatchTaskPipeline` (`rg --glob "**/*.test.ts*"` returned no matches), leaving cross-module task handoff shaping and settled-result behavior unverified.

No test hits were found for `resolveShotImagesEditorProps` and `prefetchAdjacentGenerationPages`/`cleanupDistantGenerationPages`, despite both being seam-heavy coordination code with runtime collision/cleanup behavior.

Boundary helpers use divergent failure contracts across similar integration paths: `generationTaskBridge` has Result-returning internals plus throwing wrappers, `useLineageChain` swallows failures into empty data, and `clipManager/metadata` converts errors to duration `0`.

Metadata boundary normalization is defined in `toGenerationMetadata(...)` but `transformForTimeline` bypasses it via direct cast `(shotGen.metadata as GenerationMetadata | undefined)`, making seam behavior depend on unchecked shape assumptions.

Task parameter models are wide multi-era bags instead of explicit variants: `JoinClipsTaskParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/joinClips.ts` and `IndividualTravelSegmentParams` in `src/shared/lib/tasks/individualTravelSegment.ts` mix legacy/new/orchestrator fields in one interface.

Task-param interpretation is duplicated across layers: `src/shared/lib/taskParamsUtils.ts` (deriveInputImages/derivePrompt fallback chains) and `src/shared/components/TaskDetails/VideoTravelDetails.tsx` re-implement similar fallback traversal over `orchestrator_details`, `full_orchestrator_payload`, and `individual_segment_params`.

Multiple thin wrappers/adapters add indirection without much behavioral leverage, e.g. `handleAddToShotResult` + `handleAddToShotAdapter` patterns in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts`.

UI behavior duplication exists for annotation controls in both `src/tools/edit-images/components/InlineEditView.tsx` and `src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx` with near-parallel button logic and diverging styling literals.

Some local control-flow paths mask failures with sentinel values (e.g. `handleCreateShotAdapter` returning `{ shotId: '', shotName: '' }` in `src/tools/travel-between-images/components/ShotImagesEditor/hooks/useShotImagesEditorCallbacks.ts`) instead of explicit error signaling.

Inline style/class complexity is high in lightbox/layout orchestration (`src/shared/components/MediaLightbox/components/layouts/LightboxLayout.tsx`), with many branch-specific UI states and hardcoded style choices.

findings.summary: Overlay open/close ownership is fragmented by repeated wrapper-controller setup across UI modules.
evidence: Each component receives controlled `open/onOpenChange` (or equivalent) and then locally creates `useOverlayState(...)` before passing through to Dialog/Tooltip primitives.
This creates repeated per-component lifecycle policy (close behavior, suppression flags, handler indirection) instead of a clear single ownership boundary for overlay state.
findings.suggestion: Adopt one explicit overlay contract at the architecture level: either fully controlled primitives (no local overlay controller wrappers) or a shared overlay adapter layer with standardized behavior, then migrate these components to that single pattern.

findings.summary: Generation row type authority is split between a legacy facade and domain types, weakening ownership clarity.
evidence: `src/types/generationRow.ts` declares itself a compatibility facade and aliases `GenerationRowDto` to `GenerationRow`.
Domain entities such as `Shot` use `GenerationRow` from domain type paths, so two architectural locations can appear canonical for the same conceptual type.
findings.suggestion: Define one canonical exported `GenerationRow` type surface (domain-first), and rename legacy/transport aliases explicitly (for example `GenerationRowLegacyDto`) so compatibility paths cannot be mistaken as canonical ownership.

findings.summary: Single types barrel mixes domain and transport contracts, obscuring integration seams.
evidence: index.ts exports both domain-facing types (GenerationRow, GenerationParams) and boundary/legacy transport types (GenerationRowDto, GenerationRowLegacyAliases, PersistedGenerationParams) from one entry point.
Callers can import transport and domain contracts from the same barrel without an explicit boundary decision, increasing chance of cross-layer leakage.
findings.suggestion: Split exports by seam intent (for example, domain vs persisted/transport entry points) and keep the default barrel domain-focused so handoffs remain explicit.

findings.summary: Opaque param bags make mapper handoffs under-specified and harder to reason about.
evidence: PersistedGenerationParams and GenerationParams both define open-ended fields (orchestrator_details/orchestratorDetails and extra) as Record<string, unknown>.
Mapper entry points are present, but these unbounded structures can pass through unchanged, enabling ad-hoc boundary behavior outside a clearly typed translation contract.
findings.suggestion: Replace broad bags with focused typed sub-interfaces (or discriminated unions where variants exist) and make mapper translations explicit for each supported key group.

findings.summary: Task APIs use oversized optional param bags instead of explicit mode variants
evidence: `JoinClipsTaskParams` combines multi-clip, legacy start/end/intermediate, loop/edit/audio, and orchestration metadata in one interface with many optional fields.
`IndividualTravelSegmentParams` similarly merges current and legacy fields plus `originalParams` fallback behavior, then branch-heavy builders reconstruct payloads.
Builder logic repeatedly conditionally spreads fields to assemble payloads, indicating missing discriminated task-mode contracts.
findings.suggestion: Split each task API into discriminated unions by mode (e.g., legacy_join | multi_clip_join | video_edit_join; segment_regen_from_pair | segment_regen_from_order) and move conversion from legacy input to canonical shape into a single adapter per task type.

findings.summary: Task param interpretation is duplicated across utility and UI layers
evidence: `taskParamsUtils` already defines multi-source fallback logic for prompt/images/loras.
`VideoTravelDetails` re-traverses the same nested parameter sources with local `asRecordOrUndefined`/`pickString` and separate precedence rules.
`ImageGenerationDetails` also performs independent nested metadata fallback chains for prompt/model/resolution/loras.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a canonical `TaskParamsViewModel` mapper in one module and make details components consume that typed view model, removing per-component fallback trees.

findings.summary: Generation-task integration seam is split across overlapping modules
evidence: `generationTaskBridge` handles mapping/preload/cache enrichment of task associations.
`useTasks` separately owns task querying/polling/filtering mechanics and freshness signaling.
`useGenerationMutations` contains its own generation scoping and cache mutation patterns, creating multiple places to reason about generation↔task consistency.
findings.suggestion: Create a single generation-task repository layer (query + mutation + cache policy) and have hooks delegate to it, so project scoping, mapping, and invalidation contracts are centralized.

findings.summary: Callback APIs mix rich results and lossy adapters, obscuring failure semantics
evidence: `useShotImagesEditorCallbacks` defines `CallbackResult<T>` and detailed result handlers, then wraps them with boolean/object adapters (`handleAddToShotAdapter`, `handleCreateShotAdapter`).
`handleCreateShotAdapter` returns empty identifiers on failure, collapsing actionable error state.
Downstream UI wiring expects simple callback shapes, so errors become hidden at boundary seams.
findings.suggestion: Standardize boundary callbacks on one result contract (prefer `Result` shape) and let UI callsites map errors to UX explicitly rather than returning sentinel values.

findings.summary: Annotation button behavior is duplicated with diverging implementations
evidence: Both files implement shape-mode toggle + delete overlays with parallel selection-position logic and near-identical control sets.
The duplicated implementations already diverge in styling/token usage (theme classes vs hardcoded color classes), increasing drift risk.
findings.suggestion: Extract one shared `AnnotationActionOverlay` component with styling variants and reuse it in inline edit and lightbox layouts.

findings.summary: Realtime instrumentation monkey-patches global console.warn at runtime
evidence: `installRealtimeInstrumentationImpl` replaces `console.warn` and inspects message strings to trigger reconnect behavior.
This creates hidden global side effects and string-coupled control flow unrelated to callsite intent.
Retry/polling policy already exists in `DataFreshnessManager`, so recovery logic is split between explicit state handlers and implicit console interception.
findings.suggestion: Replace console interception with explicit realtime status/event hooks that publish typed signals to reconnect/polling coordinators.

findings.summary: Task orchestration contract/pipeline seam logic is untested
evidence: `buildOrchestrationContract` performs field translation and optional inclusion decisions that define cross-layer payload shape.
`runBatchTaskPipeline` coordinates validation, fan-out, all-settled aggregation, and optional lifecycle callback invocation.
Search over test files found no direct tests for either API (`rg --glob "**/*.test.ts*"` for both symbols returned no matches).
findings.suggestion: Add focused unit tests that lock contract serialization rules and pipeline behavior for mixed success/failure batches, callback invocation ordering, and empty/invalid input edge cases.

findings.summary: Critical UI coordination boundaries rely on untested runtime behavior
evidence: `resolveShotImagesEditorProps` throws at runtime on key collisions while dynamically merging multiple prop groups.
`prefetchAdjacentGenerationPages` sequences prefetch + cache cleanup and invokes side-effect preloading/eviction services.
No direct tests were found for `resolveShotImagesEditorProps`, `prefetchAdjacentGenerationPages`, or `cleanupDistantGenerationPages` in `*.test.ts*` files.
findings.suggestion: Add seam-level tests for prop-collision failure mode and for adjacent-page prefetch/cleanup order, including error callback behavior and distant-page eviction correctness.

findings.summary: Similar integration helpers expose incompatible failure semantics
evidence: `generationTaskBridge` defines Result-returning internals but public helpers throw on failure (`getPrimaryTaskIdForGeneration`, `getPrimaryTaskIdsForGenerations`).
`useLineageChain` logs and returns `{ chain: [], isPartial: false }` on failure, collapsing error into empty-data semantics at the hook boundary.
`loadClipDuration` logs and returns a synthetic success value (`durationSeconds: 0`) when metadata extraction fails.
All three consume the same `OperationResult` pattern but convert failures differently at module boundaries.
findings.suggestion: Standardize boundary semantics (either explicit Result through the seam, or throw-and-handle at one orchestration layer) and document a single policy for fallback vs surfaced failure.

findings.summary: Timeline transform bypasses metadata guard and uses unchecked cast
evidence: `generationMetadata.ts` provides `toGenerationMetadata(...)` as the boundary normalizer for unknown metadata payloads.
`transformForTimeline` assigns `metadata` using direct cast `(shotGen.metadata as GenerationMetadata | undefined)` instead of normalization.
This makes the transformer accept arbitrary metadata shape without an explicit seam validation step.
findings.suggestion: Use `toGenerationMetadata` (or a stricter shared parser) in `transformForTimeline` and define behavior for invalid metadata (null out, sanitize, or raise controlled error).

findings.summary: Timeline positions hook keeps duplicate legacy APIs despite claiming one source of truth
evidence: useTimelinePositions return type and return object expose positions plus displayPositions/framePositions/setFramePositions aliases marked for backwards compatibility.
The same migration-era compatibility facade pattern appears in src/types/generationRow.ts, indicating legacy overlap has been retained rather than retired.
findings.suggestion: Introduce a deprecation removal phase: keep only `positions`/`updatePositions` internally, migrate remaining callers off alias members, then remove compatibility fields and the wrapper setter in one cleanup pass.

findings.summary: Browser hooks leak NodeJS timeout types, creating inconsistent low-level timer handling
evidence: useOperationTracking and useTemporaryVisibility type timeout refs as NodeJS.Timeout | null in client hooks.
useStableSkeletonVisibility uses window.setTimeout and number | null, which is the browser-native shape in the same subsystem.
findings.suggestion: Standardize timer refs in browser hooks to `ReturnType<typeof window.setTimeout>` (or `number`) and consistently use window timer APIs to remove Node type coupling.

findings.summary: Typed event helpers assume window exists, unlike nearby browser-safe utilities
evidence: dispatchAppEvent and listenAppEvent call window.dispatchEvent/addEventListener directly with no `typeof window` check.
supabaseSession in the same shared layer protects non-browser execution paths before touching window/localStorage.
findings.suggestion: Add a small browser-runtime guard helper in typedEvents (no-op or safe unsubscribe in non-browser environments) and route all window access through it.

findings.summary: Network status ownership is split between `services` and `lib` via a shim file.
evidence: src/shared/lib/NetworkStatusManager.ts contains only a pass-through export from `@/shared/services/network/networkStatusManager`.
Within the same `shared/lib` package, other files (for example ProjectScopedCache) are concrete implementations, so package role alternates between implementation and compatibility alias.
findings.suggestion: Choose one stable layer for network status ownership (prefer `shared/services/network`), migrate imports to that canonical path, then remove or isolate the shim in an explicit compatibility/barrel location.

findings.summary: `Slot` is a UI composition primitive stored in the generic `lib` package.
evidence: src/shared/lib/Slot.tsx is React-specific UI composition code (`React.forwardRef`, `React.cloneElement`, event/style/className prop merging).

src/shared/lib/ProjectScopedCache.ts represents generic data/cache utility logic; both living in `shared/lib` indicates a mixed package boundary (UI primitives mixed with generic domain-agnostic helpers).
findings.suggestion: Move `Slot` into a UI-focused package (for example `src/shared/components/ui/Slot.tsx` or `src/shared/components/primitives/Slot.tsx`) and keep `shared/lib` for non-visual utilities.

findings.summary: `useContentResponsive` advertises content-area semantics but is organized and implemented as viewport-only logic.
evidence: Hook docs claim behavior is based on available content area and pane reductions, but implementation only reads `window.innerWidth` and `window.innerHeight`.
Tests validate only viewport resize behavior, so package/API intent suggests a broader responsibility than the module actually owns.
findings.suggestion: Either rename and document this module explicitly as viewport-responsive (for example `useViewportResponsive`) or relocate/extend it near layout/pane state so it truly models content-area boundaries.
